title: cacalia alliariae subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Cacalia alliariae subsp. pyrenaica is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Pyrenean garlic milkwort. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Cacalia alliariae subsp. pyrenaica is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be used in various herbal preparations, including salves and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
